about: Senior data platform engineer who builds infrastructure that actually works. Spent the last few years optimizing pipelines, migrating warehouses, and convincing ml models to behave in production. Particularly good at making real-time systems that don’t collapse under load and explaining why vendor lock-in is a trap.
headline: Senior data engineer @ Hinge | real-time ml infrastructure
location: New York, New York, United States

education.activities_and_societies:
- Survived university honors program & gave suspiciously upbeat tours to confused high schoolers (husky ambassador).
- Hackathon enjoyer (hackbeanpot, huskyhacks); good at building “minimum viable demo” and even better at pretending it worked.

education.description: Problem Solving, Time Management and +2 skills
education.activities_and_societies:
- Founded debate team, 2014 state champion (MSDL); led varsity math and peer-tutoring ops.
- Swam competitively and feigned “leadership” on student council. If you were decent at math in MA, you probably knew me.

experience.description:
Data, platform, ai.
- Migrating legacy redshift warehouse to databricks, consolidating pipelines, cutting costs, unlocking ml workloads.
- Giving ai engs the dopamine hit of "fresh" model features instead of "whenever batch god wills it".

Data platform.
- Enabled growth engs to launch personalized flows; cut event latency 95%+ so ops could react while users were still awake.
- Ported 100+ key dbt models to new cloud-native ELT, slashed incident downtime from 7d to 8h; established multi-vendor patterns (bc vendor lock-in sucks).

Data science & analytics.
- First data engineering hire; stood up full-stack data infra (spark, delta lake, airflow, looker); putting infra on actual rails; feedback loop in hours not days.
- Reduced deploy time 95%, dropped incident count, wrote rfcs, ran 1:1s, did perf reviews, technical leadership.

Health data platform.
- Implemented real-time flink on kinesis ingestion, 50x latency improvement.
- Orchestrated ml deploy + inference (docker, lambda, 200 req/s); cold starts be damned, the models shipped.

Cloud network infrastructure.
- Implemented rollout prioritization for config updates on backbone (b2) routers, keeping the internet stable even when engineering wanted to move fast with global changes.

additional_sections.content:
Recent posts and reposts include commentary on:
- Security for AI agents in production and support for Polymorph (YC W26).
- New York City economic fundamentals and housing affordability.
- TiE Young Entrepreneurs (TYE Boston) high school entrepreneurship program.
- Reflections on a Chicago tech trek organized by Atlas Digital Consulting Group.
- Endorsement of Vikram Kaushik for new grad SWE roles and his events with Figma and MongoDB internships.
- Personal feature in Business Insider about bicoastal living between NYC and SF.
- Ramp San Francisco office opening and Ramp’s Series D-2 funding round and AI tools.

(Only high-level themes captured; individual reaction/comment counts are visible per post.)
